During Fool's Gold "Holy Ship" rap party in January 2014, Pharrell originally premiered a snippet of an unreleased Fabolous song where Loso was heard singing Kendrick's "Alright" hook. Not a "remix" in the common sense, as it shares almost the same instrumental as the jazz rendition of Kendrick's song by Terrace Martin. Loso would leak his "Alright" version on SoundCloud three months after To Pimp A Butterfly release. The track was accompanied with a music video directed by Gerard Victor on YouTube.

Madlib told in an interview to The Guardian that Kendrick wanted to work with him on To Pimp A Butterfly, but the collab did not materialize, as Madlib was busy. “I was busy on my own thing. Missed opportunities, man.”. Collaborating with Lamar “probably wouldn’t have worked out anyway”, he says, “cos I’m like a sore thumb.”.

During i-D Magazine conversation with Baby Keem, Kendrick mentions that Keem stole one particular beat marked in red of his DJ Dahi beats list from his drive, which originally was supposed to make up on DAMN. but Kendrick didn't know what to record over it. A year later after the interview launch, Keem would reveal that "16" was the beat he stole on his Twitter. Keem made the song when he started reworking on DIE FOR MY BITCH.

Kendrick used emojis to mark songs from his "Look Woman" tracklist for further development. Unlike the polished "Everybody Sensitive," this project consisted mostly of unfinished sketches and melodies (like "Style") that fit a specific vision. Many of these ideas, including "We Cry Together," were eventually reworked or integrated into the 2019 "Everybody Sensitive" tracklist.
Google Sheets version of a .xslx file for the finalized "Everybody Sensitive" tracklist containing infornation about production, musicians, engineers, where songs were recorded, etc.
Info about the "Everybody Sensitive" tracklist, written by zaytop.
"Everybody Sensitive" tracklists, including each iteration of the tracklist, written by Kendrick.

These are lyrics written on iCloud notes by Kendrick himself and emojis to mark songs, like in "Look Woman". There are typos and mistakes such as with autocorrect, and sometimes the lyrics aren't completely finished as to what was in the song itself. Another example of a mistake: "Fater may I" written for "Father May I".

Name (Check out the Tracker website!):
Drake - First Person Shooter
(feat. J. Cole & Kendrick Lamar) (prod. Vinylz, Boi-1da, OZ, Tay Keith, Coleman & FNZ)
(Cole or Slap?)
Notes (Join the Discord for updates and: Infodump regarding Kendrick shared by a trustworthy source mentioned Drake's "First Person Shooter" initially meant to have a feature from Kendrick, as it was J. Cole wish having all of the three on the song. Drake didn't want to send the song to him, instead he only sent the instrumental, which was because he intended to "set Kendrick up for failure" (either not using his verse or trying to outrap him). This is likely why he mentioned the song in his verse on "Like That".
